text: Nephron sparing surgery is currently the standard treatment for CT-A renal tumors and minimal invasive surgery is in vogue.
Robotic assistant has an added advantage over laparoscopy primarily in the excision, suturing and renal reconstruction but it adds to the cost.
Increasing robotic use mandates innovative solutions to optimize the OR time. increasing the turnover and offsetting the cost.
Our patient was a sixty seven year old gentleman, known diabetic, hypertensive, with chronic kidney disease and baseline serum creatinine of one point seven.
Incidentally found to have three point nine into three centimeter left lower polar renal mass. CT scan was suggestive of renal mass.
On functional scan the GFR of left kidney was twenty three point six ml per minute and of right kidney was twenty seven ml per minute.
Patient was taken up for left laparoscopy assisted robotic partial nephrectomy.
The surgical team utilizes six standard robotic ports for the procedure, positioning them strategically for optimal access during the laparoscopic-assisted robotic partial nephrectomy.
The surgeon converts the 8mm robotic ports to 5mm by closing the adapters, making them compatible for laparoscopic instruments.
The surgeon begins the procedure by using a harmonic device to reflect the left colon medially, exposing the surgical field for the nephrectomy.
The surgeon uses a harmonic device to divide the lino renal ligament, freeing the upper pole of the kidney from the spleen.

The surgeon identifies the gonadal vein and reflects the colonic mesentery medially. The ureterogonadal vein complex is lifted anterior to the swash sheath, with small vessels clipped and divided using a harmonic device.
The surgeon reaches the renal hilum and identifies the renal vein, which is then dissected and skeletonized using a combination of blunt and sharp dissection techniques.
The surgeon uses a harmonic device to further mobilize the upper pole of the kidney within the swast muscle, separating it from surrounding tissues.
The surgeon mobilizes the kidney posteriorly and laterally, completely separating it from the spleen.
The surgeon further dissects the renal hilum, clips, and divides the lumbar vein, ensuring clear separation from surrounding structures.
The surgeon identifies the renal artery located posterior to the renal vein and performs dissection using both blunt and sharp techniques to separate it from surrounding tissues.
The surgeon uses hook cautery to incise the peritumoral serosa, fascia, and fat, exposing the renal capsule and delineating an exophytic tumor.
The surgeon further dissects and skeletonizes the renal artery using a combination of blunt and sharp dissection techniques, revealing the artery's structure and surrounding tissue.
At this time the assistant is asked to get ready with SI robot which was meanwhile being used to perform a robotic prostatectomy in parallel operating room.
The renal artery and vein are lubricated separately, and the Da Vinci SI robot is docked into position for the nephrectomy procedure.
Robotic bulldog clamps are applied to the renal artery and vein separately, securing these vessels during the nephrectomy procedure.
The surgeon resects the exophytic tumor from the kidney, maintaining a bloodless field through effective hyaluron controls.
The surgeon repairs the opened pelvic lysol system using a PDS-IV-O suture, carefully stitching the tissue to restore its integrity.
The surgeon performs internal suturing on the kidney using a V-Loc suture, addressing bleeding points and extending the suture to the diametrically opposite surface of the kidney.
The surgeon performs interrupted suturing over the renal raphe using a surgical bolster, securing hemostasis with Surgiflow. The renal vessels are de-clamped, and hemostasis is visually reconfirmed.

The surgeon sutures perirenal fat over the resected area of the kidney and pegs the kidney to the abdominal wall. A drain is placed, and the specimen is retrieved using an indigenous endo-catch bag through a lateral port incision.

Total operative duration was two hundred minutes including laparoscopic console time of eighty minutes and robotic console time of sixty minutes. Warm ischemia time was twenty-four minutes fifteen seconds and the blood loss was less than fifty mL per minute. Histopathology revealed papillary carcinoma with clear margins.

To conclude, hybridized laparoscopy assisted robotic partial nephrectomy consistently achieve an output of three cases in optimal times in ROR setup, optimizing the results, reducing times, increasing outputs and balancing surgeon's fatigue.
The qualitative benefits accrue in terms of efficiency, reduce surgeon and team fatigue as well as preserving the minimal invasive benefits. Thank you.
